faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It carries a ₹1,000 + taxes joining and annual fee, but the annual fee is waived if you spend ₹1,00,000 or more in the preceding year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: Yes, for online shoppers who regularly transact on its 10 partner brands (Amazon, Flipkart, Myntra, Swiggy, Zomato, Uber, etc.) and can stay within the ₹1,000/month cashback cap — the 5% rate is among the best uncapped-brand rates available on an entry-level card.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Salaried millennials and young professionals with online-shopping-heavy spending patterns who can comfortably clear ₹1,00,000 in annual spend to waive the fee.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: No. It is issued on the Visa network, and Visa credit cards cannot currently be linked to UPI apps in India — only RuPay credit cards support UPI linkage.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Only conditionally. Since December 2023, free domestic lounge access is no longer automatic; you must spend ₹1,00,000 or more in a calendar quarter to earn a lounge-visit voucher as one of two milestone reward options.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: HDFC generally requires a net monthly income above ₹35,000 for salaried applicants, or an ITR above ₹6 lakh per annum for self-employed applicants.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Heavy spenders who will blow past the ₹1,000/month cashback cap quickly, or anyone who wants guaranteed lounge access without hitting a spend threshold.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: Amazon Pay ICICI Credit Card (uncapped 5% on Amazon for Prime members, lifetime free) or SBI Card SimplyCLICK (10x rewards on select online brands) are worth comparing.
